title: Product Design with AI Constraints
concept: Designing LLM products requires planning around non-deterministic outputs: the UI must guide user expectations and handle latency gracefully.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need to design products differently when they are powered by AI?

options.text: AI responses are always instant and identical.
AI responses take time to generate and can change each time.
AI apps never need loading states or instruction text.

exercises.explain: Unlike traditional databases, AI models take time to think (latency) and produce unique responses each time (non-determinism). This requires unique UI designs.

cards.title: The Rules Have Changed
cards.body: Traditional apps are predictable: click a button, and the exact same page loads instantly. But AI is different! It takes time to think, and its answers change every time. How do we design apps for an engine that is unpredictable and sometimes slow? Let's find out!

cards.title: Designing with Constraints
cards.body: Designing for AI constraints means building user interfaces (UI) that accept two big truths: AI outputs are non-deterministic (they change slightly every time) and they take time to generate (latency). Good design sets realistic expectations and keeps the user engaged during the wait.

cards.title: The Unpredictable Chef
cards.body: Think of an AI app like a restaurant with a talented but unpredictable chef. Instead of promising a picture-perfect dish that looks exactly the same every single time, the menu warns you that 'dishes are hand-crafted and may vary.' The waiter also brings you bread and water to keep you happy while the chef cooks. In design, we use helper text and loading animations to do the exact same thing!

cards.title: AI Design in Action
cards.body: When you ask ChatGPT a question, it doesn't leave you looking at a blank screen. It uses a streaming text effect (typing word-by-word) and shows suggested prompt ideas. This tells you the AI is actively thinking and guides you on what to ask next.

cards.title: Myth: Bad UI Causes Latency
cards.body: Many people believe that if an AI tool is slow or gives unexpected answers, the UI design has failed. In reality, AI models are naturally slow and creative! The design succeeds by setting the right expectations, showing progress bars, and making it easy for users to edit the results.

cards.title: Traditional vs. AI-First UI
cards.body: See how designing with AI constraints in mind creates a much better user experience than treating AI like a traditional database.

right.text: A streaming typing effect with placeholder tips, followed by easy buttons to 'Regenerate', 'Make Shorter', or 'Edit'.

left.text: A static loading spinner that freezes the app, followed by a rigid block of text that cannot be edited or regenerated.

exercises.prompt: Which UI pattern is best suited for handling the latency (waiting time) of an AI generating a long article?

options.text: Keep the screen completely blank until the entire article is finished.
Use a streaming text effect to show the words as they are being generated.
Trigger a popup error message if the generation takes longer than 2 seconds.
Force the user to re-type their prompt to speed up the system.

exercises.explain: Because AI takes time to generate complete responses, displaying the text as it is generated (streaming) keeps the user engaged and proves the app is working.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Because AI outputs can change every time, designers should provide tools like 'Regenerate' or 'Edit' to help users refine the results.
exercises.explain: AI is non-deterministic, meaning the same prompt can yield different outputs. Designing helpful UI (like edit or regenerate buttons) helps users manage these variations.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of AI behavior:
exercises.sentence: Because AI outputs are [blank], the user interface must help guide user expectations when results vary.
exercises.explain: Because AI is non-deterministic, its outputs are not always identical. Good UI design helps users understand that they might get different results each time they try.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about AI product design is FALSE?

options.text: Loading skeletons and typing animations help manage user patience during latency.
UI designers should guarantee that the AI will always return the exact same output.
Providing example prompts helps guide users to get better results from the AI.

exercises.explain: The second statement is incorrect because AI responses are naturally variable (non-deterministic). Good design must embrace and plan for this variance rather than trying to hide it.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are the Lead Designer for a new AI writing assistant. Which strategy best adapts your product's user interface to the natural constraints of Large Language Models?

options.text: Force the AI to always output the exact same sentence and load in under 0.1 seconds.
Hide all loading states and let the app look frozen until the AI is completely done.
Design supportive UI states that manage wait times and guide users on how to handle varied AI outputs.
Remove all user input fields and replace them with fixed, pre-written buttons.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Designing for AI requires moving away from the rigid, instant, and perfect patterns of traditional software. We must design for waiting times (latency) and unexpected or varied answers (non-determinism) by guiding user expectations.
